title: Farmhouse Potting Bench
summary: A tall-backed potting bench with mirrored 2x4 leg sets, a 2x6 slatted worktop and shelf, and a scribed 2x2 X brace on the back — a different lumber system than this catalog's other potting benches.
description:
This potting bench uses a different lumber system than this catalog's other two: 2x6 for the slatted worktop and bottom shelf instead of 1x stock, with a genuinely tall 2x4 back rising well above the work surface to give the bench real presence against a shed or fence wall. Build the two leg sets as true mirrors of each other, not identical twins — checking that early is what keeps the whole bench from ending up lopsided once the back and top go on.

The back is added in two passes: a 2x4 cross-piece attached 1-1/2" down from the tops of the 2x6 rear legs (reinforced with an angled screw from above for extra hold), and then a separate top 2x4 cap, measured and cut to fit your actual assembled width rather than a plan number. Between those two passes, the X detailing gets scribed directly against the framed opening and cut on a compound miter saw — the same scribe-don't-calculate approach this catalog uses on its X-braced furniture, and for the same reason: it absorbs small differences in how square your particular frame came out.

Space the 2x6 worktop slats with real gaps when you attach them from underneath — the gaps are what let potting soil fall through instead of piling up on the surface, the same functional detail as this catalog's other potting benches, just executed here in heavier stock.

cutList.note: Cut to fit your actual assembled width.

steps.title: Build the leg sets
steps.body: Build the two leg sets with two screws per joint and glue. Build them as true mirrors of each other, not identical copies.

steps.title: Join the leg sets
steps.body: Attach the two leg sets together using the 2x2 rails, with screws and glue, on a level surface.

steps.title: Add the lower back rail
steps.body: Measure up 1-1/2" on the back legs from the tops of the 2x6 worktop position. Attach the 2x4 lower back rail with screws, adding an angled screw from the top of the rail into the back legs for extra hold.

steps.title: Add the bottom shelf
steps.body: Attach the 2x6 slats inside the bottom shelf opening. Add a center support board across the tabletop span to give the tabletop boards something to attach to in a later step.

steps.title: Add the top back rail
steps.body: Measure and cut the top back rail to fit your actual assembled width. Attach with screws and glue along the top edge.

steps.title: Scribe and cut the X brace
steps.body: Scribe the 2x2 X detailing directly against the framed back opening and cut on a compound miter saw. Attach the X pieces with glue and a screw or brad nail at the center crossing.

steps.title: Add the tabletop and finish
steps.body: Space out the tabletop boards with gaps for drainage and attach from underneath. Attach the top shelf to the tops of the legs and the back top piece with glue. Sand and finish.

unresolvedImages.alt: Tall farmhouse potting bench with a scribed X-braced back
